description: Merge conflict failed at tier ai-resolve. Branch: overstory/frontend-builder/frontend-repo-integration. Agent: frontend-builder. Conflicting files: .

name: Inline chat activity with shared provider wiring
description: Render AI SDK assistant parts in transcript order and enable tool-call streaming so read/edit actions appear inline in chat instead of only in the footer activity indicator. Normalize provider creation on the server so chat, orchestrator, and key validation share the same endpoint and header behavior.

name: split-panel-chat-state
description: Split-panel chat state must scope preview files by panel and enforce one live panel per conversation; conversation file persistence should save and restore panel-scoped preview plus changeset state, because shared preview state or shared runtime chat ids cause cross-panel state bleed.

name: proposal-approval-ui
description: When repo change proposals are surfaced via propose_changes, the chat UI should detect the latest unanswered proposal from tool data or persisted assistant text and offer an accept action above the input that sends the approval message programmatically instead of requiring typed approval.

name: Queued chat follow-ups
description: Queued follow-up prompts are maintained in hook-local queuedMessages state for both useChat and useOrchestrator; ChatInput sends immediately when idle, queues on Enter or the queue button while streaming, and auto-dispatches the next queued item after streaming stops.

name: proposal-approval-gate
description: In src/hooks/useChat.ts, pause the active run immediately after propose_changes resolves and hard-gate repo edit tools until the user explicitly approves the proposal to prevent staged changes before acceptance.

name: changes-inspector-sidebar
description: Use the shared right rail as a dual-view workspace: open a Changes inspector from the header line totals, keep generated repo edits unstaged by default, and require explicit per-file or bulk staging before enabling PR creation.

name: repo-proposal-auto-approve
description: Repo proposal approval now supports a persisted auto-approve mode in settings: useChat should still require propose_changes first, but when autoApproveRepoChanges is enabled it should mark the proposal approved immediately, continue with repo edits without pausing, and return assistant text that marks the proposal auto-approved so pending-proposal UI stays hidden.

name: conversation-switch transcript reset
description: Chat panels should remount and clear AI runtime transcript state when the selected conversation changes; rehydrate messages from IndexedDB after resetting in-memory chat state so switching away and back does not resurrect stale assistant step rows.

name: sidebar hover pin slot
description: Reserve a fixed leading slot for the thread pin control in ChatSidebar so the title width stays stable; show the pin button with opacity/scale on row hover and keep it visible when pinned.
Selecting a newly created conversation before its first send settles remounts the chat panel and can blank the transcript.
resolution: Keep a pending conversation ID local in the hook and only call onConversationCreated after append/orchestration finishes, so the panel handoff happens after the first exchange is stable.

name: electron preload bundle path
description: electron-vite preload output may be emitted as out/preload/preload.mjs rather than out/preload/index.js; Electron main should resolve the existing preload bundle dynamically instead of hardcoding one filename.

title: Preview rail opens on explicit intent
rationale: Artifact generation should stage preview files without stealing focus; restoring a conversation should respect the saved workspace open state instead of assuming files imply visibility.

name: proposal pause snapshot persistence
description: When propose_changes pauses a live AI SDK run, persist the current assistant snapshot before stop() so the approval CTA and read_repo_file tool rows survive remounts and new-thread handoff.

name: composer context usage meter
description: Render per-conversation context usage in the composer chrome using the active provider/model from the current chat hook, not just global settings, so the percentage and token totals match the live session.

title: Gate reasoning effort to supported OpenAI models
rationale: The installed OpenAI adapter only accepts reasoningEffort values of low, medium, or high for reasoning-capable OpenAI models. Exposing the selector for unsupported providers created a misleading no-op control, so the UI now hides it unless the backend can forward the setting.

name: Per-panel context usage status
description: Chat panels publish ephemeral context-usage snapshots keyed by panel id so shared chrome like the footer can show provider, model, and used/total context for the focused panel without prop-drilling chat state through AppLayout.

name: Electron chrome styling
description: The Electron shell uses separate rounded sidebar and main-content surfaces with subtle borders, blurred backgrounds, compact pill buttons, and a denser title row so the sidebar toggle, thread list, and top bar feel closer to the Codex desktop app without changing chat behavior.

name: Settings workspace and flat shell styling
description: The Electron shell should avoid heavy outer shadows on large rounded panels; prefer flat bordered surfaces for the sidebar and main pane. The Settings modal should use a desktop workspace layout with a clear header, tab rail, provider list, and card-based configuration sections instead of a dense single-column form.

name: electron preload output path
description: In electron/index.ts, wait for electron-vite preload output and prefer out/preload/preload.mjs before creating BrowserWindow; falling back to index.js can break preload injection and hide the embedded API port from the renderer.

name: tab-switch preserves chat mount
description: Keep ChatPanelContainer mounted and hide it with CSS when AppLayout switches away from the chat tab; unmounting the chat tree aborts in-flight orchestrator streams and drops AI SDK proposal state.

name: openclaw chat adapter
description: Integrate OpenClaw in CloudChat through the local openclaw CLI with --agent main --local --session-id <conversationId> and translate the completed JSON payload into a one-shot AI SDK data stream response for the existing useChat frontend.

title: OpenClaw excluded from orchestrator roles
rationale: Expose OpenClaw as a main-chat provider only and filter it out of orchestration provider pickers because the current orchestrator/server path depends on AI SDK model adapters and per-provider model selection, while OpenClaw executes through its own CLI/runtime contract.

name: chat-panel runtime split
description: ChatPanel should switch between regular chat and orchestrator by rendering separate child runtime components so each mode owns its own hook graph instead of invoking both hooks from the same component.

useChat selected Hermes toolsets with useHermesStore((s) => s.getEnabledToolsets()), which returns a fresh array on every render and trips React's cached getSnapshot requirement for useSyncExternalStore.
resolution: Subscribe to useHermesStore((s) => s.toolsets) and derive the enabled toolset list with useMemo so the selector output stays referentially stable unless toolset state actually changes.

name: openclaw gateway-backed provider
description: CloudChat's OpenClaw provider should mirror openclaw-dashboard-pro by invoking the openclaw agent command without the local flag, syncing requested model refs through openclaw models set, and sourcing model options from openclaw models list/status instead of a static placeholder list.

title: Persist discovered OpenClaw models in settings state
rationale: OpenClaw model discovery is runtime data from the CLI, not static app metadata. Storing discovered model refs in settings state lets the setup wizard, provider settings screen, and composer model picker stay consistent while the server remains the source of truth that applies the selected model before each turn.

name: settings-store deep merge
description: Normalize and deep-merge persisted settings state in src/stores/settings-store.ts so partial localStorage snapshots do not replace the full providers map, which avoids undefined config fields and preserves defaults for newly added providers like openclaw and hermes.

name: renderer api port fallback
description: When Electron embeds the API server, pass the selected API port to the renderer via URL query params in electron/index.ts and let src/lib/api.ts read that param as a fallback when preload is unavailable, so the renderer still targets the embedded server instead of defaulting to localhost:3001.

title: Hermes defaults use tool-capable OpenRouter models
rationale: The local Hermes bridge sends OpenRouter tool calls whenever Hermes toolsets are enabled, so the old Hermes free model default produces opaque provider failures instead of usable agent runs.

name: chat-area stream auto-scroll
description: Chat auto-scroll should track the last rendered message digest and Hermes tool activity digest instead of only the messages array reference, because streamed providers can mutate the last message in place while still needing the viewport pinned to the bottom.

name: chat hermes error recovery banner
description: Hermes/OpenRouter compatibility failures in ChatArea should render as an inline recovery card with direct model-switch actions and a settings shortcut, so users can resolve unsupported tool-call models without leaving the chat flow.

name: hermes repo runtime fallback
description: Hermes requests with an active repo must resolve to the OpenRouter runtime rather than the local Hermes bridge, because repo-attached analysis depends on the repo-capable tool path.

name: repo approval continuation
description: Repo approval follow-up turns should mark the request as a continuation of the accepted proposal, remove propose_changes from the available tool list for that turn, and treat any fallback propose_changes call as already approved so the agent executes edits instead of re-planning.

name: hermes pseudo tool fallback
description: When a provider emits repo tool syntax as plain assistant text instead of structured AI SDK tool-invocation parts, parse pseudo propose_changes/batch_edit_repo_files calls from the transcript, strip them from the visible markdown, and use the parsed payload for proposal detection plus repo changeset hydration so the workspace and PR UI still work.

name: post-create PR review modal
description: After staging repo changes and creating a pull request, keep the modal open as a review surface that polls GitHub for check status, groups checks by provider, and offers merge controls with merge strategy plus commit title/body so users can finish the PR workflow without leaving the app.

name: Hermes approval detection
description: For Hermes repo proposals, do not memoize pending approval detection only on the messages array identity in ChatArea; the latest assistant message can be mutated in place during streaming, so proposal detection must recompute from live message content and tool invocations to keep the approval surface visible.

title: Digest-cache Hermes proposals
rationale: Hermes can mutate the latest assistant message in place while streaming proposal tool data. Caching pending proposals behind a content digest keeps approval detection live for mutated messages while avoiding fresh-object render loops in ChatArea and useChat.

name: Hermes approval dialog fallback
description: ChatArea should auto-open a blocking approval dialog for pending repo proposals, and proposal detection should fall back to transcript copy like 'Here's a proposal...' when structured propose_changes payloads are missing.

outcomes.test_results: vitest proposed-changes/chat-approval/change-approval-modal passed; eslint on touched files passed; tsc --noEmit passed

name: Hermes stalled proposal fallback
description: Pending repo proposals should be derived from assistant text parts as well as message.content, and the approval modal should still open when Hermes stops after propose_changes even if auto-approve is enabled; only suppress the modal once repo continuation tools actually run after the proposal.

outcomes.test_results: vitest proposal/modal suite passed; eslint on touched files passed; tsc --noEmit passed

name: Approval banner follows shell theme
description: Repo proposal approval should render as a compact neutral banner above the composer, using background/border/muted theme tokens instead of a special green gradient so it matches both dark and light app themes.

outcomes.test_results: vitest approval suite passed; eslint on touched files passed; tsc --noEmit passed

name: Approval banner lives in transcript flow
description: Repo proposal approval should render as a compact inline banner inside the scrollable chat transcript, so it appears near the composer at the bottom but scrolls with message history instead of staying fixed above the input.

outcomes.test_results: vitest proposal/modal suite passed; eslint on touched files passed; tsc --noEmit passed

name: Welcome screen hero mark
description: The new-thread welcome screen can carry a warm branded hero mark above the existing title and repo selector, but the mark should sit inside a neutral glassy container so it feels integrated with the shell across light and dark themes.

outcomes.test_results: vitest welcome/approval suite passed; eslint on touched files passed; tsc --noEmit passed

name: Cache embedded API port in renderer
description: When the Electron renderer resolves the embedded API port from preload or the apiPort query param, persist that port in sessionStorage and reuse it as a later fallback. This avoids chat requests falling back to localhost:3001 and surfacing generic 'Cannot connect to API' retries when preload or URL state drops out.

outcomes.test_results: vitest api-base-url/chat-error-banner passed; eslint on touched files passed; tsc --noEmit passed

name: approval continuation survives tool steps
description: Approval follow-up runs in src/hooks/useChat.ts must keep continuing_approved_proposal in the hook-level request body across AI SDK tool-call resubmits, and only clear repo edit approval state after a non-tool-calls finishReason. In src/components/chat/MessageBubble.tsx, collapse duplicate tool-invocation parts with the same toolCallId so call/result updates render as a single repo activity row.

name: restart recovers pending proposal approval
description: In src/hooks/useChat.ts, approval follow-up detection must recover from persisted transcript state after a restart by treating a plain approval phrase like 'go ahead' as continuing_approved_proposal whenever findPendingProposal(messages) returns a saved assistant proposal. In src/components/chat/ChatArea.tsx, suppress the approval banner and accept action when conversationId is null so stale proposal transcript cannot spawn a new draft thread.

name: repeat provider failures resurface per turn
description: In src/components/chat/ChatArea.tsx, dismissed provider errors must be cleared when a new user message arrives so the same runtime failure message can surface again on later sends instead of leaving the transcript looking blank. In server/index.ts, Hermes chat failures that bubble up as 'Cannot connect to API' should be rewritten to a bridge-specific message so missing hermes-bridge is diagnosable from the chat UI.

name: repo verification before PR
description: Staged repo changes should pass through a provider-agnostic verification step before PR creation: clone the base branch, apply staged file edits, run install plus available lint/typecheck/test/build scripts, and surface the results in the PR modal with an explicit override path for failures.

title: clear staged repo state on repo switch
rationale: Repo-bound staged changes and cached repo file contents must be cleared when a panel switches to a different active repository or exits repo mode, otherwise PR review and verification can target stale files from the previous repo session.

name: repo rail per conversation
description: Treat the right workspace rail as the per-panel repo surface: when a repo is selected, open the rail on a repo tab, scope the file tree/cache/selected file to the panel conversation state, and reuse that same cache for both visible file browsing and read_repo_file tool calls.

name: panel-scoped repo workspace
description: Selected GitHub repos should load into the panel-scoped workspace rail as a repo tab, with the repo tree, cached file contents, and selected file stored in the panel changeset so split panels and restored conversations stay isolated.

name: repo tree hydration for repo chat
description: Repo-mode chat turns should hydrate the selected repo file tree before append and pass it as repo_file_tree to the chat backend so provider prompts can identify candidate files without asking the user to share paths.

name: repo explorer rail over preview
description: The right workspace rail should prefer a VS Code-style repo explorer and code viewer over a live preview surface: keep repo tree navigation and change review in the rail, and treat preview output as hidden so the panel can evolve into a full editor later.

name: workspace rail resize parity
description: The right workspace rail should use the same clamped drag-resize interaction as the main sidebar, with repo and file headers wrapped in min-w-0 truncate containers so long repository names and file titles do not bleed into the split view.

name: repo attach keeps rail collapsed
description: Selecting a repository should set the workspace rail's preferred view to repo without opening it; the explorer stays opt-in until the user explicitly opens the right rail.

name: repo read path guard
description: Before executing read_repo_file in repo chats, normalize the requested path against the loaded repo tree and return actionable suggestions for nearby real paths when the model guesses a missing file. Surface those failures explicitly in the transcript instead of treating short error strings as successful reads.

name: hermes-repo-read-approval
description: In repo chat mode, gate read_repo_file behind an accepted propose_changes step so Hermes cannot start repo exploration before approval; keep repo-attached chrome copy from implying edits are already approved.

name: minimax-empty-delta-role
description: When using MiniMax through the OpenAI-compatible SDK, sanitize SSE chunks that emit delta.role as an empty string by rewriting that field to assistant before streamText validation.

name: hermes-curated-model-picker
description: Limit Hermes model selection UI to the curated tool-capable shortlist (Llama 4 Maverick, GPT-4.1 Mini, Gemini 2.5 Flash) and do not prepend saved non-curated models back into the dropdown options.

name: proposal-continuation-per-request
description: Pass continuing_approved_proposal only on the explicit approval continuation append calls, not in the default useChat request body, or fresh repo turns can lose propose_changes due to stale hook body state.

name: repo-read-before-approval
description: Allow read_repo_file before proposal approval so analysis and repo-inspection prompts can explore files; keep edit/create/delete tools approval-gated instead of locking reads.

name: repo hydration progress
description: Repo workspaces should distinguish tree indexing from file-content caching: store repo tree load status per panel, show cached-file progress against the indexed tree in shared chrome, and explain that selecting a repo indexes paths while file bodies load on demand.

Panel store selectors used with React subscriptions must not synthesize a fresh object on every read. Normalizing state in getters is safe only when the stored object is incomplete; otherwise return the original reference or wrap the selector with shallow memoization to avoid useSyncExternalStore snapshot loops.
resolution: Return the original panel changeset reference once it already contains the full shape, and use shallow-memoized selectors in PreviewSidebar for panel preview and changeset objects.

name: repo read path gating
description: Client-side read_repo_file should reject placeholder root paths and block arbitrary GitHub content requests until the selected repo tree has been indexed. Once the tree is available, validate requested paths against it and return tree-guided errors instead of raw 404s.

name: branch-resolved repo reads
description: GitHub repo hydration should resolve the selected branch to its tree SHA before requesting the recursive tree, and read-file requests should pass the selected branch ref explicitly. This keeps repo indexing and file reads aligned even when branch names or default refs are not handled implicitly by the API.

name: structured provider error normalization
description: Frontend chat surfaces should normalize unknown provider errors by extracting nested message/error/detail fields before rendering banners or logging. This prevents opaque fallback strings like [object Object] from reaching the UI and keeps provider failures actionable.

name: streaming edit line metadata
description: MessageBubble should derive per-file change metadata from live repo tool args or staged changes so chat tool rows can show affected line counts and line ranges while repo edits are still streaming.

outcomes.test_results: npm test -- src/test/message-bubble.test.tsx; ./node_modules/.bin/eslint src/components/chat/MessageBubble.tsx src/lib/change-diff.ts src/test/message-bubble.test.tsx

name: repo loading locks composer
description: ChatArea should disable the composer and new-thread suggestion actions while a selected repo tree is still loading, using the per-panel repoFileTreeStatus from the changeset store so users cannot start repo-mode turns before the file tree is ready.

outcomes.test_results: npm test -- src/test/chat-area-repo-lock.test.tsx; ./node_modules/.bin/eslint src/components/chat/ChatArea.tsx src/components/chat/ChatInput.tsx src/components/chat/WelcomeScreen.tsx src/test/chat-area-repo-lock.test.tsx

description: Repo attach can appear stuck because the footer and explorer use cached file-body count (repoFileCache) as a progress metric after repoFileTreeStatus is already ready; users see values like 1/791 even though tree indexing completed and only on-demand file caching remains.
resolution: Report tree indexing status separately from cached file bodies, e.g. show 'repo indexed' once setRepoFileTree marks the tree ready and label repoFileCache as cached/opened files instead of load progress.

content: Repo workspace status copy should distinguish full tree indexing from on-demand file-body caching: use 'indexed' for repoFileTree readiness and 'cached' for repoFileCache counts to avoid implying the repo is still loading.

In the chat sidebar, the top-right action next to 'New thread' should be a single settings icon that opens the settings modal, and the redundant footer settings row should be removed to keep the sidebar chrome compact.

name: repo tool validation error summaries
description: Repo tool validation errors should be normalized before rendering in chat: when batch_edit_repo_files schema validation reports missing fields inside changes[n], surface a concise summary like 'change N is missing path, action' instead of dumping the raw JSON payload and zod issue array.

title: Use xAI Chat API for local tool flows
rationale: CloudChat's existing chat runtime depends on client-side AI SDK function tools such as repo and file operations. xAI's Responses API supports server-side tools and cannot be mixed into that flow directly, so the low-risk Vercel integration path is the Chat API via the official xAI provider or the OpenAI-compatible adapter. Responses API should be isolated to separate server-side research/agent routes if needed.

name: Hermes tool activity uses inline tool rows
description: Hermes bridge tool_activity events should be merged into the same inline tool-invocation chat UI as normal AI SDK tool calls. Strip Hermes bridge markdown activity text from assistant content and preserve running-event args when completed events omit input so action rows dedupe cleanly.

name: Long-running task budgets
description: For hour-scale coding work, keep orchestrator SSE alive during planning and synthesis and use configurable long-running timeouts for orchestrator subtasks, Hermes provider/tool execution, and OpenClaw turns instead of short fixed caps.

name: Shared app logo asset
description: Use src/assets/cloudchat-mark.svg as the source of truth for branding, render WelcomeHeroMark from that asset, and regenerate Electron/macOS icons plus the browser favicon with npm run icons.

name: Local agent startup guidance
description: When Hermes Bridge or OpenClaw is selected, surface startup instructions both in provider settings and in chat error banners, and normalize server-side runtime failures into provider-specific guidance instead of generic connection errors.

name: Shared uploaded logo asset
description: When swapping branding, replace src/assets/cloudchat-mark.svg and regenerate build/icon.icns, build/tray-iconTemplate.png, and public/favicon.png with npm run icons so the desktop app, tray, and web favicon stay in sync.

name: PNG master icon source
description: Use src/assets/android-chrome-512x512.png as the master branding image for Electron/macOS icon generation, then regenerate build/icon.icns, build/icon.png, and public/favicon.png via npm run icons.

name: PNG-driven macOS icon generation
description: Generate Electron/macOS app icons directly from src/assets/android-chrome-512x512.png so npm run icons no longer depends on a separate tray SVG; ship build/icon.icns for the app bundle and build/icon.png for the runtime dock icon.

name: macOS icon badge compositor
description: Render the macOS app icon from the raw flame mark by compositing it onto a rounded gradient badge with shadow in scripts/render-macos-icon.swift; keep tray and favicon outputs on the raw mark so only the app icon gets the 3D-style background treatment.

name: Hermes pseudo repo edit staging from text parts
description: Hermes repo edits can arrive as pseudo tool calls inside assistant text parts while message.content stays empty. Reconstruct assistant text from both sources before extracting pseudo repo tools so staged changes and commit UI appear.

name: streaming scroll intent
description: ChatArea should disable auto-scroll immediately on upward wheel or touch intent during streaming; relying only on post-scroll near-bottom checks lets new chunks snap the transcript back to the bottom and makes scrolling feel locked.

name: electron approval attention api
description: Electron approval prompts should notify through a preload-exposed attention API and only fire once per pending proposal; the main process should decide whether to show a macOS notification and dock bounce based on window focus and visibility.

name: dedicated macos tray template asset
description: Generate the macOS tray icon from a dedicated monochrome padded template asset instead of scaling the full-color app mark directly; keep the tray image template-flagged and resize it to menu-bar height in Electron.

name: hermes repo stop recovery
description: When Hermes ends a repo-mode turn with finishReason stop immediately after read_repo_file and there is still no proposal or staged edit, treat it as stalled repo work and auto-continue with a targeted follow-up prompt instead of resetting the repo workflow state.

name: hermes pseudo edit chunking
description: Hermes bridge should stream pseudo repo tool payloads in large chunks instead of 20-character slices so batch_edit_repo_files does not flood SSE and make repo-edit turns appear stuck.

name: repo-mode hermes tool gating
description: When a repo is attached, Hermes should strip local terminal/files/code-execution toolsets and recover plain filename-plus-code-fence responses into staged repo edits so supported models cannot bypass read_repo_file and batch_edit_repo_files.

name: Hermes proposal pause reset
description: Clear stale proposal pause state when a user responds to a pending repo proposal, and only pause Hermes proposal detection for truly unapproved proposals so approved continuation runs and assistant message-id churn do not re-trigger stop().

name: Freeze streaming repo request body
description: Freeze the useAIChat request body at send, auto-continue, and regenerate time during repo-mode turns so read_repo_file cache updates do not mutate repo_file_cache or repo_file_tree on the active streaming request and trigger render loops.

name: Hermes bridge approved repo continuation
description: Hermes bridge repo continuations should keep looping after read-only acknowledgement stops: process any returned tool_calls regardless of finish_reason, and for approved repo turns that already used read_repo_file but not repo edit tools, inject one bounded continuation message that forces actual repo tool use instead of narration.

name: Hermes repo stop recovery after partial edits
description: Repo-attached Hermes turns should auto-continue when the latest assistant message stalls after read_repo_file even if earlier approved edits are already staged, otherwise Maverick can halt mid-plan after partial repo progress.

content: Keep the left sidebar thread-focused: remove GitHub, Analyzer, and Knowledge quick-nav from the shell, keep the main chat pane as the only primary view, and surface GitHub/Knowledge configuration inside Workspace Settings instead.

name: Hermes final repo read recovery
description: Hermes repo stop recovery should inspect the last repo workflow tool in order and auto-continue when an assistant turn ends on read_repo_file, even if earlier repo edits happened in the same message.

content: Hermes assistant markdown code fences should render through the shared chat code-block shell with the native React copy button, rather than the Shoelace copy-button variant, so Hermes model outputs visually match the rest of chat.

name: Hermes markdown code blocks collapse when long
description: Long Hermes markdown code fences in chat should use the shared code-block shell with a collapsed default height and inline Show more/Show less controls, matching the existing chat diff preview pattern.

name: Chat code blocks use editor chrome
description: Shared markdown code fences in chat should render with a dedicated editor-style shell: VS Code-inspired title bar, file tab, gutter, dark syntax surface, and the existing copy/collapse controls layered into that chrome rather than a generic card.

name: Inline diff previews share editor shell
description: Repo edit previews in chat tool rows should reuse the shared editor-style shell and render their gutter and diff rows as separate fixed columns so line numbers stay aligned with the visible code rows.

name: sidebar hover controls collapse
description: In src/components/sidebar/ChatSidebar.tsx, keep thread titles at full width by collapsing hover-only pin, panel, and delete controls to zero width at rest and expanding them with width, opacity, and translate transitions on hover.

name: new thread button hover icon
description: In src/components/sidebar/ChatSidebar.tsx, the New thread button should keep its label readable at rest and reveal the leading plus icon by expanding a collapsed icon slot on hover instead of reserving permanent space for the icon.

name: sidebar settings cog hover spin
description: In src/components/sidebar/ChatSidebar.tsx, the settings icon should perform a single eased hover spin with a slight opacity lift by making the button the hover group and transitioning the cog's transform instead of using a continuous animation.

name: repo-issue-browser-workflow
description: Repo issue work should open from a sidebar-adjacent modal that searches repositories, paginates issues at 25 per page, supports clone or fork working copies, and carries fork PR base metadata into repo chat and PR creation.

name: Hermes footer tool counts
description: ChatArea footer tool counts should match visible Hermes activity rows by counting the latest assistant message's structured tool calls, pseudo repo tools, text-file edit fallbacks, and in-flight tool_activity events instead of only completed structured calls.

name: PR verification workspace detection
description: Repo verification should choose the nearest package.json to the changed files, run install and validation commands from that workspace, and label command output with the workspace path so nested app changes like client/src/App.tsx do not skip checks.

name: Centered PR verification loader
description: CreatePRModal should replace the inline verification spinner with a centered blocking overlay that reuses the sidebar GhostIcon, shows staged verification phases, and animates a bounded progress bar while review-and-check requests are in flight.

name: Hermes curated model shortlist
description: Hermes settings should ignore bridge-discovered availableModels and always fall back to the curated tool-capable shortlist so stale persisted model lists cannot hide meta-llama/llama-4-maverick from the picker.

name: Hermes approved stop recovery
description: When an approved Hermes repo turn ends with finishReason stop before any repo tool runs, keep the approved continuation alive, auto-continue with continuing_approved_proposal, and suppress the conversation-level approval UI when Allow all is active.

name: Hermes approved read-stop recovery
description: Hermes approved repo continuation should auto-continue after a read_repo_file stop even if cached transcript state still makes the previous proposal appear pending; gate read-stop recovery on the approved-run refs rather than pendingProposal alone.

title: Hermes repo runtime stays on bridge
rationale: Repo-attached Hermes turns should stay on the local Hermes bridge instead of being rerouted to raw OpenRouter so the bridge can run its repo tool loop, pass repo headers, and recover read-only and approval stalls for llama-4-maverick.

name: Hermes repo tool enforcement retry
description: When llama-4-maverick drifts into repo-mode prose or code fences without any tool calls, the Hermes bridge should append a strict tools-only correction and force the next call onto propose_changes or required repo tools so the turn behaves like a code agent instead of narrating.

name: Hermes approved read-only continuation
description: Hermes approved repo turns should auto-continue after any read-only stop, not just acknowledgement phrasing, so llama-4-maverick can reach repo edit tools without depending on a separate frontend retry.

name: Hidden Hermes auto-continue prompts
description: Hermes repo auto-continue recovery should append hidden system messages instead of visible user prompts so bounded recovery runs do not clutter the transcript or look like user interruptions.

name: Hermes activity text falls back to tool rows
description: When Hermes streams repo activity as blockquoted status text, MessageBubble should strip those lines from markdown content and rely on inline tool rows with a glimmer treatment so read/thinking feedback feels active instead of duplicated.

name: create-pr-modal-footer
description: CreatePRModal uses a dedicated footer outside the scroll region so Cancel and Create PR remain visible while long review content scrolls inside the modal body.

name: repo edit intent classification
description: Repo-attached chat turns should classify the latest user request as read-only vs edit intent, thread that intent through useChat/server/Hermes, and only enter propose_changes or edit enforcement for explicit modification requests.

name: strip malformed repo payload dumps
description: Chat transcript cleanup should strip malformed repo edit argument dumps that leak as HTML/JSON paragraphs with code fragments, even when they are not valid pseudo tool calls, so MessageBubble only shows the real assistant text and repo tool rows.

name: server-backed chat persistence
description: Back the renderer chat store with the embedded Express server and a local SQLite file, while keeping src/lib/db.ts as the stable interface and falling back to legacy IndexedDB when the server is unavailable; use Electron CLOUDCHAT_USER_DATA_DIR for the production DB path and in-memory SQLite under Vitest.

name: count-aware hermes edit status
description: When Hermes streams repo tool activity without stable per-file inputs, route toolActivity into the footer ActivityIndicator and derive edit copy from staged changes so the chat UI can say Editing N files and list the staged paths instead of appearing stuck on a single generic edit row.

name: Hermes repo read-stall enforcement
description: Recent repo-tool phase detection must treat assistant tool-call messages without explicit role fields as assistant turns, and server-side enforcement should keep forcing repo tools when the latest repo workflow step is read_repo_file even if the same turn already staged edits.

name: Sanitize Hermes text parts like message content
description: Hermes transcript cleanup must apply the same malformed payload and blockquote-status sanitization to assistant text parts as to message.content, and leaked repo payload detection should decode HTML entities and strip embedded parameters/description blobs even when they are appended after legitimate prose.

outcomes.test_results: npm test -- src/test/pseudo-tool-calls.test.ts src/test/message-bubble.test.tsx; npx eslint src/lib/pseudo-tool-calls.ts src/components/chat/MessageBubble.tsx src/test/pseudo-tool-calls.test.ts src/test/message-bubble.test.tsx

name: approval-banner-scroll-sync
description: ChatArea auto-scroll should rerun when the inline approval banner becomes visible, because proposal detection opens the banner on a follow-up render; keep the chat flex stack min-h-0 so the approval card stays above the composer and scrolls like a transcript message.

name: hermes-approved-read-stop-budget
description: For approved Hermes repo turns, reset the short stop-stall counter whenever a new repo progress digest appears (tool name plus path), but keep a larger total auto-continuation budget so multi-file read phases can survive more than two stop-after-read cycles without looping forever.

name: diff line totals use true edit counts
description: Use getChangeLineDelta for staged diff totals and activity stats so equal-length line replacements count as both added and removed instead of netting to zero.

title: Split first-party provider factories from compatible transport
rationale: cloud-chat-hub should instantiate google, xai, groq, deepseek, mistral, together, and cerebras through their first-party Vercel AI SDK adapters while keeping model discovery explicit and preserving Hermes on its dedicated bridge validation path. This avoids treating every provider as a generic OpenAI-compatible runtime and keeps Hermes behavior isolated.

name: conversation-scoped background chat runtime
description: Keep chat runtime mounted per panel while backgrounding only streaming conversations, and key repo/preview state reads by conversation scope instead of panel id so switching threads does not abort or leak edits into the newly selected thread.

title: Agentic defaults stay single-key compatible
rationale: Enable orchestrator by default with OpenAI gpt-5.4 for both planner and coder roles, because the current setup flow captures one provider key while orchestrator requires credentials for both roles. Raising default max tokens and sub-agent count preserves the more agentic preset without breaking fresh installs.

title: Planner can follow the selected chat provider
rationale: The orchestrator planner should default to the user’s active chat provider/model instead of staying pinned to a separate hardcoded provider. Store a follow-selected-provider flag so planning/thinking tracks the active provider by default while still allowing users to toggle follow mode off and pin a separate planner.

name: chat-store idempotent persistence
description: Chat-store persistence should treat missing conversation file state as empty data and make repeated message POSTs idempotent so renderer restore and assistant snapshot writes do not spam 404/409 errors.

name: sidebar footer repo status
description: Sidebar footer repo status should read from the focused panel changeset and render the repo full name with indexed/cached file counts, keeping the repo name as the truncating segment so the counts stay visible.

name: sidebar footer compact mode
description: The sidebar footer should collapse into a compact mode once the resizable sidebar gets narrow: shorten or hide the permissions label, switch the repo label to the short repo name, and keep only the indexed file count visible so the footer remains a single clean line without count truncation.

title: Sidebar footer omits repo counts
rationale: The lower-left footer becomes visually noisy and degrades at narrow sidebar widths when it repeats indexed and cached file counts. Keep those counts in the top bar and reserve the sidebar footer for a compact repo identity pill only.

title: Repo access labels use GitHub permissions
rationale: Repo chrome should not show placeholder permission text. When an attached repository exposes GitHub permission flags, derive a user-facing access label from them: admin maps to Admin, push maps to Can push, pull-only maps to Read-only, and missing permission data falls back to Access unknown until the repo is reattached with permission metadata.

name: sidebar header icon collapse
description: When the resizable chat sidebar reaches a narrow width, the top bar should tighten its gap and convert the New thread control into an icon-only button while preserving the New thread accessible label. This avoids multiline wrapping and keeps the narrow sidebar header visually balanced.
MiniMax-compatible chat turns can fail with error 2013 when the server prepends system-role messages into the chat message array.
resolution: Normalize system prompts into the dedicated streamText system field and strip system-role messages from the provider message list before dispatch, including hidden auto-continue system messages.

description: MiniMax M2.5 rejects chat payloads that include a system role; CloudChat currently forwards both the default system prompt and hidden auto-continue system messages through streamText's system field, which the compatible adapter sends upstream as a system message.
resolution: For MiniMax providers, do not send streamText's system field; instead remap the prompt into an allowed role or provider-specific instruction channel before the request is serialized.

name: issue-browser chat scope handoff
description: Issue-browser repo workflows must respect chat scope IDs: plain repo attach should target the active conversation scope, while issue-driven explain/fix actions should first reset the panel to a draft thread, attach the repo under the panel scope, then queue the prompt so useChat creates a fresh repo-scoped conversation.

name: request-time repo body preparation
description: Repo-mode useChat requests must prepare the chat body at send time via the AI SDK's experimental_prepareRequestBody hook, because mutating a ref before append() is not enough to update the SDK's last-render body. Reading the current changeset and GitHub PAT from stores during request preparation preserves activeRepo, repo_file_tree, and github_pat for fresh repo turns and continuations.

name: repo issue chat handoff disables orchestrator
description: Repo issue new-thread chat handoffs should disable orchestrator before queueing the repo prompt so explain/fix flows always land in standard repo chat with repo intent handling instead of planner-style analysis.

name: Issue explain handoff intent override
description: Issue explanation chats should set repoEditIntentOverride=false on queued panel prompts, because issue titles and bodies often contain edit words like 'fix' that would otherwise flip the repo handoff into change mode.

name: approval UI after stream stop
description: ChatArea should defer Hermes proposal approval banners and attention notifications until streaming has stopped, so content-matched proposal detection does not surface approval UI mid-stream.

name: read-only repo handoffs stay out of proposal mode
description: Read-only repo issue analysis turns should expose only repository read tools, and when GitHub access is unavailable the system prompt should fall back to issue-text analysis without web search, repo tool retries, or change proposals.

name: Parse AI data streams for repo events
description: Hermes/useChat stream interception must parse AI SDK data-stream lines, not only legacy SSE data: frames, so server-executed repo tool calls and repo_file_* events are recognized early enough to avoid duplicate client-side repo tool execution and to keep changeset state in sync.

name: Render tool-only assistant turns
description: When AI SDK multi-step assistant turns include only step-start parts plus direct toolInvocations, MessageBubble must still render the direct tool rows; otherwise approved repo continuations can appear as blank assistant messages even though repo tools ran.

name: hermes-draft-pr-and-encoded-contents
description: Hermes repo chat flows should URL-encode GitHub contents paths and preserve the PR draft flag end-to-end from CreatePRModal through github-integration so staged chat changes can open draft PRs and operate on files with reserved characters.

name: read-only-issue-prompt-intent-guard
description: Repo intent classification should honor explicit read-only guards like explanation-only and do-not-change instructions before strong edit keywords, so issue-analysis prompts with words like fix or changes do not push Hermes into edit mode.

name: Issue explanation CTA
description: Repo issue explanation chats should preserve issue metadata on the active repo and surface an inline Fix issue in chat CTA after the assistant completes a read-only explain turn; the CTA should queue a repoEditIntentOverride=true follow-up prompt in the same chat so the model can move from root-cause analysis into proposing and staging the fix.

name: repo-issue-chat-github-context
description: Repo-attached issue explanation and repo chat turns must include github_pat and the selected repo default branch in the request body so server-side repo tools can inspect the correct repository state.

name: issue-browser-inline-create
description: RepoIssueBrowser now supports inline GitHub issue creation from the issue list pane via a server-backed create-issue action, then selects the created issue so existing attach, explain, and fix flows continue without leaving the modal.

name: issue-browser explain selected repo handoff
description: Repo issue browser explain actions should hand off the selected issue repo directly so read-only issue analysis switches chat context even when no local working copy exists.

name: repo issue handoff preserves panel repo during conversation reset
description: When a repo issue action starts a new thread from an existing conversation, mark the panel for repo handoff preservation so the blank-thread transition clears transcript state without wiping the attached repo before the new conversation is created.

name: issue explanation dual follow-up CTA
description: Issue explanation follow-ups should branch inside chat: after a read-only explain turn, keep the repo-attached CTA visible with separate actions for drafting a GitHub issue update (repoEditIntentOverride=false) and starting the fix flow (repoEditIntentOverride=true), so the same issue context can stay read-only or switch into edits without a new repo handoff.

name: repo issue activity graph from commit history
description: Repo issue browser activity should derive its 30-day graph from recent commit history rather than relying only on GitHub stats/commit_activity, because GitHub stats can be unready for external repos and hide the graph. The server route should bucket commit dates over the last 30 UTC days and return deterministic counts for public or accessible non-owned repos.
